text.title: Hybrid Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Hybrid Delivery. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every workstream MUST declare its mode (predictive OR agile) with a one-paragraph rationale. Implicit hybrid is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit mode causes mode drift; explicit declaration anchors governance and reporting.

rule.statement: Inter-workstream handoffs MUST be defined as contracts with inputs, outputs, acceptance criteria, SLA, and escalation owner.
rule.rationale: Without contracts, the agile workstream blames the waterfall workstream for late inputs and vice-versa; both miss commitments.

rule.statement: Program governance MUST run as a single layer over both modes. Parallel governance per mode is forbidden.

rationale.source: PMI Agile Practice Guide; PRINCE2 Hybrid

rule.rationale: Parallel governance creates conflicting decisions and doubles meeting load; one layer with mode-aware sub-reports prevents fragmentation.

rule.statement: Reports MUST translate workstream metrics into a common dashboard (e.g. agile velocity → cost+schedule equivalent; predictive EVM → throughput equivalent).
rule.rationale: Without translation, stakeholders see incomparable metrics and lose program-level visibility.

rule.statement: Workstream cadences MAY differ internally, but MUST align at handoff dates. Mis-aligned cadences violate the program timeline.
rule.rationale: Mis-aligned cadences cause handoff misses that ripple downstream; alignment forces explicit synchronisation.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Hybrid Delivery fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Hybrid Delivery outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
